system: You are a helpful programming assistant with deep knowledge of systems.

messages.content: Explain the entire Linux kernel boot process in detail, from BIOS POST to init system startup.
The Linux kernel boot process involves several stages: BIOS POST, bootloader (GRUB), kernel decompression, kernel initialization, and init system startup. Let me walk through each stage in detail.
Now explain how the scheduler works, including CFS, real-time scheduling classes, and how context switching is implemented on x86_64.
The Linux scheduler uses the Completely Fair Scheduler (CFS) as its default scheduling class. CFS uses a red-black tree of virtual runtimes to ensure fair CPU time distribution.
Can you compare this with the Windows NT scheduler and explain the key architectural differences?
